ruleTodos.name: Backend and frontend endpoint contracts must be inspectable
ruleTodos.description: Frontend and backend endpoint definitions should be structured so they can be extracted, compared, and validated automatically by inspection scripts.

ruleTodos.name: Avoid inline styles
ruleTodos.description: Inline styles should be avoided except for dynamic runtime-calculated styling that cannot reasonably live in CSS classes.

ruleTodos.name: DTOs must be responsibility-specific
ruleTodos.description: DTOs should represent a specific use case or operation and should avoid becoming large generic transport objects reused across unrelated operations.

ruleTodos.name: Frontend pages should separate rendering from orchestration
ruleTodos.description: DOM rendering, state management, navigation, API communication, and interaction orchestration should be separated into focused modules/helpers when frontend complexity grows.

ruleTodos.name: Do not hardcode SQL outside repositories
ruleTodos.description: SQL statements must only exist in repositories or dedicated persistence infrastructure.

ruleTodos.name: Inspection scripts must separate rule definitions from rule execution
ruleTodos.description: Rule metadata/configuration should remain externalized from rule execution logic so the framework can evolve toward reusable rule engines and DSL-style inspection architectures.

ruleTodos.name: Frontend must not bypass backend architecture
ruleTodos.description: Frontend code must communicate through approved API/service boundaries and must not directly access persistence, infrastructure, or internal backend implementation details.

ruleTodos.name: Mappers must remain transformation-focused
ruleTodos.description: Mappers should only perform structural/data transformation and must not contain orchestration logic, persistence logic, or business decision-making.

ruleTodos.name: Entity and module names should remain semantically consistent
ruleTodos.description: Singular/plural naming, module ownership, and entity terminology should remain consistent across frontend, backend, routes, repositories, DTOs, and database structures.

ruleTodos.name: Repositories should avoid business orchestration
ruleTodos.description: Repositories should focus on persistence operations and data retrieval/update logic and should not coordinate multi-step business workflows or application orchestration.

ruleTodos.name: Routes should remain transport-focused
ruleTodos.description: Route handlers should focus on HTTP transport concerns such as request reading, validation coordination, DTO mapping, authentication/authorization integration, and response sending, while delegating business workflows to services.

ruleTodos.name: Scripts should separate configuration, execution, and reporting
ruleTodos.description: Script configuration, execution logic, runtime state, and reporting/output generation should remain separated to support reusable script infrastructure and future framework evolution.

ruleTodos.name: Services should coordinate use cases, not infrastructure
ruleTodos.description: Services should orchestrate business use cases and coordinate repositories, validators, domain operations, and infrastructure abstractions without becoming infrastructure implementations themselves.

ruleTodos.name: Validators should remain rule-focused
ruleTodos.description: Validators should focus on validation concerns only and should not perform orchestration, transport formatting, persistence coordination, or business workflow execution.
